case_name: Rosario v. Our Lady of Consolation Nursing & Rehabilitation Care Ctr.

summary.brief: Rosario v. Our Lady of Consolation Nursing & Rehabilitation Care Ctr.. Medical malpractice case involving obstetrics gynaecology.

evidence_index.text: Weinstein found a superficial bullae, a fluid-filled blister, which had no pus, was slightly warm to the touch, and also slightly tender.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Yurberg acknowledged that upon the decedent's admission to OLOC on May 11, 2009, it was noted that she had, inter alia, a stage II ulcer between the 4th and 5th toes on her left foot and cellulitis on her lower left extremity.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: The hospital's admission notes on May 30, 2009, reflect that the decedent's chief complaints, as relayed by her daughter, were that she 'developed a fever today while at nursing home,' and had a change of mental status earlier that day.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: Yurberg's careful parsing of the 'reasonable' and 'fair' standards of medical certainty does not appear to be accidental.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

evidence_index.text: DiLorenzo's consistent findings that Khokhar did not depart from accepted medical practices or that any alleged departures were not the proximate cause of the decedent's injuries or death, to a reasonable degree of medical certainty.
evidence_index.speaker: Court finding

initial_state.chief_complaint: Fall at home resulting in neck trauma
initial_state.history_of_present_illness: Patient admitted to Good Samaritan Hospital on April 30, 2009, after a fall at home. Developed swelling and redness in the left lower extremity during hospitalization.

focused_exam.skin: Superficial bullae on left lower extremity, slightly warm and tender
focused_exam.neurological: Oriented x 3 during examination by Khokhar

initial_state.initial_working_problem: Neck trauma and potential infection in left lower extremity
critical_findings: Swelling and redness in left lower extremity

requestables.name: Complete Blood Count (CBC)
requestables.description: Blood test to evaluate overall health and detect a variety of disorders

reveal.decision_impact: Did not prompt further investigation for infection

requestables.name: Cervical Spine and Rib X-rays
requestables.description: Imaging to assess neck and rib pain

reveal.decision_impact: No further action required based on imaging

timeline_phases.name: Initial Hospital Admission
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted to Good Samaritan Hospital after fall

key_events.event: Admission and initial treatment for neck trauma and cellulitis

timeline_phases.name: OLOC Admission and Care
timeline_phases.description: Patient admitted to OLOC for rehabilitation

key_events.event: Patient seen by Khokhar for neck and rib pain

decision_points.clinical_context: Patient at OLOC with complaints of neck and rib pain
decision_points.prompt: What additional assessments should be conducted given the patient's condition?

options.description: Order comprehensive skin assessment and nutritional evaluation
options.clinical_reasoning: Given the patient's risk for pressure ulcers and nutritional deficits, a comprehensive assessment is warranted.

options.description: Focus only on neck and rib pain with x-rays and CBC
options.clinical_reasoning: Limited assessment focusing on immediate complaints without addressing underlying risks.

actual_action_defendant.description: Defendant focused on neck and rib pain with x-rays and CBC
actual_action_defendant.reasoning_stated: Defendant addressed immediate complaints as requested by family

expected_action_court.description: Comprehensive assessment should have been conducted
expected_action_court.standard_of_care_basis: Expert testimony indicated need for broader assessment given patient's condition

explanation.why_defendant_wrong: Failure to conduct comprehensive assessment led to missed opportunities for early intervention
explanation.what_should_have_happened: Comprehensive assessment could have identified and mitigated risks for pressure ulcers and nutritional deficits
explanation.legal_standard_applied: Standard of care as established by expert testimony

patient_outcome.description: Patient developed multiple complications including pressure ulcers, sepsis, and pneumonia, leading to death

key_findings: Khokhar's limited role did not constitute a breach of duty
No proximate cause established between Khokhar's actions and patient's death

malpractice_determination.counterfactual: Comprehensive assessment could have mitigated risks

factual_timeline.event: Patient admitted to Good Samaritan Hospital after fall
Patient transferred to OLOC for rehabilitation
Patient seen by Khokhar for neck and rib pain
Patient transferred back to hospital with multiple complications

court_findings.finding: No breach of duty by Khokhar
